local_evidence.observed_risk: CLI JSON preview with --include-prompts could print a long key-like token from prompt history.
local_evidence.post_fix: The same preview class returned containsRawSkToken=false and containsRedaction=true.

candidate.problem: json_prompt_preview returned borrowed PromptRecord values, so opt-in stdout previews emitted risky prompt text verbatim.
candidate.risk: Even local-first prompt scans can leak sensitive prompt text into shell logs when --include-prompts is used.
candidate.decision: Clone preview records and redact risk-pattern text only in the CLI JSON prompt preview.

red.evidence: The new test failed because preview text contained the synthetic long token instead of the redaction marker.

green.evidence: The focused test passed after CLI preview records cloned and redacted their text field.

full_verification.evidence: 10 UI helper tests passed, Vite build passed, 38 Rust library tests passed, 14 CLI tests passed, doc-tests passed, and strict clippy passed.

post_fix_smoke.evidence: containsRawSkToken=false, containsRedaction=true, and the configured-limit warning was present.
